data.title: Bank Of Canada Governor Says Policy Rate Is Near The Lower End Of Neutral Range
data.description:
Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem said the central bank is at the lower end of the neutral interest-rate range, roughly at a level that can restrain inflation.

According to Jin10, Macklem described the current setting as broadly appropriate for suppressing inflation.

data.title: Bank Of Canada Governor Says Inflation Remains Well Above Target
data.description: Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem said inflation remained clearly above the central bank’s target and described Canada’s economy as weak. According to Jin10, Macklem said inflation was noticeably higher than the target and that economic conditions in Canada were soft.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Fed Chair Kevin Warsh Says Central Banks Should Decide Whether AI Is Inflationary
data.description: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh said it should be up to central banks to determine whether artificial intelligence is inflationary. According to Jin10, Warsh made the remarks when addressing the question of whether AI should be viewed as having inflationary effects.

data.title: Hangmo Technology Raises Nearly RMB 100 Million in Angel Round
data.description:
Hangmo Technology has completed an angel financing round worth nearly RMB 100 million, backed by Zhongtou Wanfang, Sanxian Technology, Beihang Tianhui, Chengmei Capital, Sanhe Investment and Changzhou Jinkong, according to 36Kr. Delta Capital served as the company’s long-term strategic financing adviser, and the proceeds will be used for product R&D and market expansion.

Founded in 2022, Hangmo develops intelligent variable-stiffness joint technology and consumer-grade smart exoskeletons, with core technology originating from a key R&D program at Beihang University’s Robotics Institute. The company’s core product is its variable-stiffness intelligent joint module, FlexmoJoint. Hangmo also said it launched an outdoor-focused smart knee exoskeleton, IRMO M1, in 2025 and that the product’s crowdfunding has reached RMB 7 million.

data.title: Mixed Premarket Moves for U.S.-Listed Chinese Stocks: XPeng Up Over 3%, NIO Down Over 2%
data.description: U.S.-listed Chinese stocks were mixed in premarket trading, with XPeng Inc. up more than 3%, Tencent Music Entertainment up more than 1%, Weibo up nearly 1%, and PDD Holdings up 0.09%, according to 36Kr. NIO was down more than 2%, while NetEase fell more than 1%; Alibaba slipped 0.69%, JD.com fell 0.49%, and Baidu declined 0.34%.

data.title: US Big Tech Stocks Mixed Premarket; Meta Up Over 5%, Nvidia Down Over 1%
data.description: US large-cap tech stocks were mixed in premarket trading, with Meta up more than 5% as of publication, according to 36Kr. Microsoft, SpaceX and Netflix rose more than 1%, while Google gained 0.27%. Arm fell more than 2% and Nvidia slipped more than 1%. Tesla was down 0.52% and Apple fell 0.26%.

data.title: Bank Of England Governor Bailey Says Rate Hold Was Based On Economic Slowdown
data.description: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey said the decision not to raise interest rates was based on a slowing economy. According to Jin10, Bailey linked the rate-hold decision to signs of weaker economic momentum.

data.title: Bank Of America Expects Bank Of Canada To Hold Rates Through 2027
data.description:
Bank of America said it expected the Bank of Canada to keep interest rates unchanged through 2027, even if the U.S. Federal Reserve resumes raising rates.

According to Jin10, Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem faced a different set of conditions than many other central bank leaders, with Canada’s core inflation measures near 2% and energy-rich regions seeing short-term gains from a surge in oil prices.

The report said the economy appeared to show no signs of overheating. After a slight contraction in the first quarter, the economy was expected to post annualized growth of more than 2% in the second quarter.

It added that traders had gradually pulled back earlier bets that the Bank of Canada would raise rates by the end of this year.

data.title: Bailey Speech in Sintra Draws Focus as UK Inflation Is Expected to Peak
data.description:
Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey is expected to face less pressure when he speaks in Sintra this week, as falling global oil prices have eased near-term inflation concerns.

According to Jin10, the Bank of England had recently faced a policy dilemma over how to balance a weak UK labor market against the threat of rising inflation.

Institutional research expected inflation to peak at 3% and then fall to 2.3% next year, which it said was well below the Bank of England’s earlier expectations. The research also expected this backdrop to allow the central bank to cut its policy rate to 3.5% from 3.75% by June 2027.

Markets are watching whether Bailey will effectively endorse that view in his remarks today and explicitly rule out the possibility of further rate hikes.

data.title: Binance Prediction Markets Integration Upgrade Will Temporarily Suspend Services
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform will carry out a Prediction Markets integration upgrade on 2026-07-02 at 06:00 (UTC) to enhance performance and stability. The upgrade is expected to last around 1 hour, during which Prediction Markets-related services will be temporarily unavailable across the mobile app, web platform, API, and Binance Agentic Wallet. Binance said user asset security will not be affected, and existing positions and market settlement logic will remain intact. The company added that services are expected to resume automatically after the upgrade, while the actual recovery time may change depending on progress.

Affected Services and Access Points: During the maintenance window, users will be unable to access the Prediction Markets page, place trades such as buying and selling, transfer funds, view positions and assets, make claims, or inquire about market settlement information, along with other related functions. Binance said the impacted entry points include Binance App paths under Exchange > Markets > Prediction; Wallet > Home > Prediction; and Exchange > Futures > Up/Down Prediction, as well as Binance Wallet Web > Prediction. For Prediction assets specifically, access points include Binance App under Exchange > Assets > Prediction and Wallet > Home > Prediction > Assets, plus Binance Wallet Web > Prediction > Assets. Binance advised users to make any necessary trading or transfer arrangements before the upgrade period to avoid operational disruption.

data.title: Mysterious Solana project World unveiled as fully onchain prediction market
data.description:
World, a previously mysterious Solana project, has launched as a fully onchain prediction market inside the Phantom wallet and at world.xyz. The platform lets users trade event contracts tied to crypto prices and the 2026 FIFA World Cup, according to CoinDesk, with more markets on sports, geopolitics and macroeconomics planned.

World is non-custodial and uses Phantom’s CASH stablecoin for settlement, with positions, settlement and redemptions occurring onchain. Chainlink provides market data and resolution infrastructure via Chainlink Data Streams and the Chainlink Runtime Environment.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Says He Profited From Stock Market Gains
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said rising stock prices have made him a significant amount of money. According to Odaily, Trump said the stock market is going up and that everyone is making money, adding that he is also profiting.

data.title: CoreWeave Shares Fall 5.3% Premarket as Meta Plans to Expand Cloud Business
data.description: CoreWeave shares fell 5.3% in premarket trading after reports that Meta plans to develop its cloud business. According to Jin10, the move weighed on CoreWeave in early trading.

data.title: Colombia Says 2027 Budget Growth Will Trail Inflation
data.description:
Colombia’s finance minister said the country’s 2027 budget growth will be lower than the inflation rate.

According to Jin10, the minister did not provide additional details on the planned budget increase or an inflation estimate.

data.title: US Companies Added 98,000 Jobs in June, ADP Says
data.description:
Private payrolls rose by 98,000 in June after a 122,000 increase in the prior month, according to ADP Research data released Wednesday, according to Bloomberg.

The median estimate in a Bloomberg survey of economists called for a 120,000 increase.

data.title: Crypto Card Payments Top $1.5 Billion Monthly, Tiger Research Says
data.description:
Cryptocurrency card payments have surpassed $1.5 billion in monthly transaction volume, according to Tiger Research. According to NS3.AI, the firm said usage remains concentrated on RedotPay and is largely driven by emerging markets including Bangladesh, India, and Nigeria.

Tiger Research added that crypto cards have not yet reached product-market fit in developed nations, suggesting adoption remains uneven across regions.

data.title: Bitcoin opens Q3 in historical red zone after rare losing first half
data.description: Bitcoin began the third quarter after falling in both the first and second quarters of 2026, a pattern seen only twice before. According to CoinDesk, BTC fell 22.2% in Q1 and 14.09% in Q2, and was trading just above $59,000 on Wednesday. The only other years with consecutive losing quarters to start the year were 2018 and 2022, when the second half failed to recover. CoinDesk cited record outflows from U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs over the past month, low on-chain active users and rotation into AI stocks.

data.title: Micron Technology Signs Strategic Agreement With General Motors to Secure Supply
data.description:
Micron Technology and General Motors have signed a strategic agreement aimed at ensuring supply and accelerating innovation.

According to Jin10, the companies said the deal is intended to secure supply and speed up innovation.

data.title: Bank of America Notes Fewer Fed Appearances After June Policy Meeting
data.description:
Bank of America said public appearances by Federal Reserve officials fell sharply in the two weeks after the June policy meeting, which it described as a possible early sign that Fed Chair Kevin Warsh is pursuing a lower-profile communications approach.

According to Jin10, Bank of America strategists said Fed officials delivered only 12 speeches and interviews since the June FOMC meeting, including events scheduled through the end of this week.

The strategists compared that with an average of about 23 appearances over the same two-week window after Fed meetings since 2022.

They said the decline could indicate that FOMC participants are following Warsh’s preference for speaking less, but it could also reflect the July 4 holiday and the possibility that officials had no additional details to add beyond the guidance released in June.

Bank of America said it remains unclear whether the shift represents a new trend or temporary noise.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Gold Holds Steady as DXY Slips to 101.42 After ADP Data
data.description: The U.S. dollar index (DXY) edged lower in a brief move after the ADP data release, trading at 101.42. According to Jin10, spot gold showed little change and was quoted at $4,024.8 per ounce.
